item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where are the footnotes? That Sector 5 bit piqued my interest but then I couldn't find the link anywhere. Other than that, excellent skip. Upvoted.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Okay. I'll PM the author regarding it.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: I think that would count as a stylistic edit, which would need author confirmation before it can be made. Edit reverted for the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both X-raisin and some other flavor were as -6 so I made X-Raisin -5. Hope that's an acceptable edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

The stuff in this is more violent then my interpretation of Wondertainment or the Foundation would do, but I can't really downvote stuff just for not fitting my headcanon so I guess this gets upvoted for the great quality and twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pyrokinetic Pineapple is my favorite.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Talking to shaggy about this article, they mentioned that they consider this to be in-line with the Adventures In Capitalism flavor of Dr. Wondertainment. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

████████ (Archduke-Viceroy of Quality Assurance)

To this:

████████ (Archduke-Viceroy of Quality Assurance)

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!
In celebration of my first SCP reaching triple digits, I went ahead and added another test with a brand-new flavor! Thanks to everyone who read and voted.

item.title: X-Raisin :D
item.content:encoded: Added a new flavor! Big thanks to Blackfluid in chat for the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A new and exiting look at everyone's favourite phyco-WillyWonka.

You sir, are truly a visionary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Delicatessen-class is fantastic.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Outside of the last note, this didn't strike me very much as a product of Wondertainment.

Wondertainment's products either have come with a long safety spiel or are only dangerous to the user when used improperly. This is just candy that straight up murders anyone who uses eats it.

Everything about this feels like the product of the Factory instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't like that it was a Wondertainment product that killed people (really didn't like it), and then I read that twist. Damn. +1, and added to my headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thought the test log was quite funny. However,

+ show block
– hide block

"Why, if it weren't for the Wonderful Testing Prowess of your assuredly egg-headed Misters and Missus, who knows what harm might befall our innocent consumers?!?"

Brix, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The title was added when I touched up the tone in the endnote. Earlier it came off as 'evil cackling mastermind' where instead I meant to imply 'Naive about how the Foundation (or humans in general) works'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
████████ (Archduke-Viceroy of Quality Assurance)

Huh. Not sure if I simply skimmed past it the first time or if it was added later, but this could clear up some things if it isn't Wondertainment his/her self, but a management guy somewhere down the line doing the dirty work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Based on the name, I'd expected something refferencing site policy on writing humanoid SCPs. Upvoted, but I'm not sure if "syndrome" is the best choice of words in context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My headcannon is more an "it."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Concievably, they may have chosen the Foundation specifically for people that deserve to die anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how Wondertainment is taking advantage of the Foundation's rigorous testing protocols as free R&D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will willingly admit that Retrocausal Gummy Worms sounds like an Isabel idea. Will they just cause a paradox in which the only thing that made you eat the gummy worms was you actually eating the gummy worms?

also, time-travelling stranglefruits.

item.title: Re: Tagging Clarifications
item.content:encoded: Whoop! Thanks for the catch.

item.title: Tagging Clarifications
item.content:encoded:
Disclaimer: I am not the tagging staff, but there are some things that I feel ought to be said.

I noticed you use the "_s" tag. These are not used for SCPs, not to mention that the hidden tag thing is no longer necessary since they created a solution to that with jarvis updating the Tales.

Also, when you tag SCPs, make sure you include the object class (which you did not). I added the "safe" tag for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay now it looks better!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cleaned up the tone in the end note. The original came out slightly darker than I meant for it, so I made it a bit more whimsical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not a fan of the gross mischaracterization of Wondertainment. Given that "bighearted innocence" is almost their prime character trait, this is a misstep, to say the least.

This plot could totally work, though. Imagine if Wondertainment realized the Foundation always tests their products, and instead of abusing this to save on costs, they interpret it as an odd form of playing with the toys. Then, Wondertainment lets the Foundation be the first to test their product, because Wondertainment thinks that'll make them happy. And you could even play up the ambiguity between whether this was an odd corporate move or a naive personal move. The concept is very workable.

That's just a thought. In the current status, this just does not coexist with other Wondertainment mythos on site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or, y'know, when he was all Factory status.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Depends. Maybe this was her father, before he went all corrupted factory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In one specific canon line, anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Required Secondary Powers not included."

Oh, this is brilliant. I love how Dr. Wondertainment used the Foundation's tendency to scientifically test everything to use them as free R&D. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This read like something a very, very too-old-for-this-shit/jaded Isabel would do, and I read it with that in mind. Not gonna lie, I want to see a story wherein she reaches that point and then goes beyond it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're corgis, actually. Corgis named "Jeremy." No mucking about with that Oompa-Loompa nonsense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She. Isabel is a lady.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He called D-Class "degenerates." If I'd been drinking something I would've spit it out at that line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't digging this until I realized Wondy's using the Foundation for product testing, and that's just great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the (SCP classification pending) would look better as a footnote. Just my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love what this implies about Wondertainment.
Firstly he sees the Foundation as an ally and is totally ignorant of how they see him. I get that some may think this is just uncharacteristic but I love that he thinks of the Foundation as "the best people for the job".
Secondly any Wondertainment scip that doesn't have an acquisition report could have been used as a beta test (which I have just headcanoned). +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You've got a point. I didn't mean for Wondertainment to come off that darkly. I've got a good idea how to salvage the mis-characterization, though. I'll try to get this fixed asap. Thanks for the input.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this idea. And since they're apparently labeled the same I could totally see a "distribution error" where the test candy gets mixed in with the final version once it's complete.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How blasé the Foundation is or is not when it comes to human experimentation is an evolving thing. While I might not necessarily agree with the interpretation presented here, where there's no second thoughts1 about feeding anomalous materials that purport to grant super powers to people, it's within site norms enough that I wouldn't characterize it as deficient writing.

All that said, I have a very difficult time believing that Wondertainment, as portrayed by almost all other writers on the site, would be okay with harming people to test its products. In most cases, on of the defining qualities of the group is that its products, while misguided and not quite cognizant of how reality works on this specific plane, are not overtly harmful or malicious. It's supposed to be a company specializing in educational products for children. They're not going to engage in crimes against humanity.

Beyond that, what sense does this make? Why would you go to the trouble of infiltrating one of the few organizations that's aware of your existence and is actively suppressing you to trick them into testing your products for you? Wouldn't it be far easier to just do the testing yourself? They clearly have no moral objections in this portrayal.

I really don't care for Wondertainment material in general, but this doesn't even really fit the themes that have been established for that group or make any sort of sense whatsoever. That might be forgivable, but there's no reason for flouting that convention in order to justify it. And without the connection to the larger mythos, I don't feel that there's much here.

Footnotes
1. Note that I wouldn't really enjoy a ten-page Ethics Committee handwringing either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad at all. +1 just for the Willie Wonka reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this idea so FREAKING much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wondering , Why the hell would wondertainment give this out to people ?
Until I realized it was a batman gambit that made the SCP test this out on D-Class.
woah, Wondertainment is Savage

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha! Now I have an image of Wondertainment testing products on Oompa-Loompa expies! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, it stands to reason that Wondertainment ought to have its own QC. However, if we suppose Wondertainment is a lone operator making these things, he might have been dishing out test products to the Foundation for beta testing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should be proud because even if this was kinda short and it almost made me downvote but when I saw the twist my jaw dropped!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is officially my first SCP! I'd like to thank Leviathan34 for looking it over in chat.

ETA. If you remember looking at an earlier iteration and gave me feedback (it was awhile ago) I'd like to thank you so lemme know(it was much duller before initial feedback).

ETA-2 (The Editing)
MTF is from Smapti's SCP-1916

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my. Nice twist! I didn't expect that! 1+
